title: Production-assurance layer: external verifier, adversarial benchmark, compliance/assurance, isolation truth model

acceptance_criteria.statement: THE SYSTEM SHALL provide an external-verifier protocol with an immutable candidate handoff and a DSSE-style attestation such that repository-local code cannot produce a production-valid VERIFIED, and candidate/policy/evidence substitution, result tampering, and stale replay are all detected.
THE SYSTEM SHALL provide an executable adversarial benchmark of at least 25 seeded cases that each drive a live AERS control and assert the expected containment.

THE SYSTEM SHALL provide a machine-readable compliance/maturity assessment that reports PASS/FAIL/PARTIAL/NOT_APPLICABLE/UNVERIFIABLE per control and detects deliberately missing controls, never marking an externally-dependent control PASS from inside the repo.
THE SYSTEM SHALL provide an evidence-linked assurance case that maps each material claim to implementation, tests, and benchmark cases and detects broken or stale claim mappings.
THE SYSTEM SHALL replace asserted isolation with an explicit truth model whose R2+ decisions fail closed when isolation is only asserted.
THE SYSTEM SHALL provide a structured threat model in which every critical threat maps to a containment control and at least one executable benchmark case, with an explicit residual-risk statement.
THE SYSTEM SHALL test the evaluator itself for seeded-defect detection, false acceptance/rejection, and reproducibility, binding results to the exact evaluator configuration.
THE SYSTEM SHALL keep installation non-destructive and idempotent and provide an inspectable migration plan and profile recommendation.

contracts: scripts/assure.py exposes: assess, profiles, benchmark, assurance, threat-model, evaluator-health, isolation, handoff, attest-demo, verify-attestation, evidence-manifest, migrate
verifier.verify_attestation(envelope, handoff) -> {valid, production_valid, verdict, reasons} and is fail-closed
assurance profiles are JSON with REQUIRED/RECOMMENDED/OPTIONAL/NOT_APPLICABLE per control

quality.non_goals: This layer never issues VERIFIED, never mutates the protected control plane, and does not deploy a production verifier (that is external).

rollout.strategy: Additive layer merged behind the existing gates; new author-side assurance targets added to make verify without weakening any existing check.
rollout.rollback: git revert of the FEAT-PA1 commits; the pre-existing control plane is untouched, so revert is clean and non-destructive.
